rocky8.10: Compile failed
lustre/llite/namei.c:2576:13: error: initialization of ‘int (*)(struct inode *, struct dentry *, struct inode *, struct dentry *, unsigned int)’ from incompatible pointer type ‘int (*)(struct inode *, struct dentry *, struct inode *, struct dentry *)’ [-Werror=incompatible-pointer-types]
 lustre/llite/namei.c:2576:13: note: (near initialization for ‘ll_dir_inode_operations.rename’)
 

 Job output URL: https://testing.whamcloud.com/gerrit-janitor/60098/results.html
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

I attempted to fix the build errors present in this patch and succeeded. Yay! Now, keep in mind I barely have any idea what I am doing, so please doublecheck the changes I made to make sure they make sense.

This service is only provided in hopes to be useful and reduce latency from push to getting actual results in the face of trivial code errors

Here's summary of my changes:
Perfect! Now let me create a summary of the fix:

## Summary

### The Fix

The compile error was caused by the removal of the `HAVE_IOPS_RENAME_WITH_FLAGS` configuration macro in the patch, which removed support for kernels before 4.0. However, the code in `lustre/llite/namei.c` still had conditional compilation directives that depended on this macro.

### What Was Changed

I modified `lustre/llite/namei.c` to remove all references to `HAVE_IOPS_RENAME_WITH_FLAGS`:

1. **Lines 32-37**: Removed the conditional macro definition for `ll_rename` that handled both with-flags and without-flags cases. Now it always uses the version with the `flags` parameter.

2. **Lines 2460-2463**: Simplified the `ll_rename` function signature to always include the `unsigned int flags` parameter, removing the conditional compilation directive.

3. **Lines 2473-2478**: Removed the conditional check around the flags validation, since the `flags` parameter is now always present.

4. **Lines 2483-2488**: Removed the conditional compilation around the `llcrypt_prepare_rename` call, always passing the `flags` parameter.

### Rationale

The `HAVE_IOPS_RENAME_WITH_FLAGS` macro was introduced to support kernel 3.14, which added the `flags` parameter to the `inode_operations->rename` function. Since the patch removes all support for kernels before 4.0, and kernel 3.14 introduced this feature, all supported kernels (4.0+) will have the `flags` parameter in the rename operation.

Therefore, the conditional compilation is no longer needed, and the code can be simplified to always expect the `flags` parameter in the `ll_rename` function signature, making it compatible with the kernel's `inode_operations->rename` interface for all supported kernel versions.

parents.subject: LU-19850 obd: Don't use obd_check_dev() in obd_import_event()

commit.subject: LU-19179 build: remove all remaining 3.X kernel support
commit.message:
LU-19179 build: remove all remaining 3.X kernel support

All kernels before 4.0 are no longer supported. Remove
the remain 3.X kernel support.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Change-Id: I4ffa3c64144682758a0ab3ef05b1021e61bc54bf
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63819
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
